commands.repair_reason: The protected maintainer label, GHSA-linked approval boundary, compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior, and missing real behavior proof make this a maintainer review item rather than an automated repair candidate.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the diff; it tightens QQBot approval authorization without changing dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, or install scripts. (sha=2a2f97ce170dd8e2a4825975f386d7fbf377b4d0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR marks QQBot no-`execApprovals` authorization as implicit, gates native fallback approval button callbacks through QQBot command-style actor authorization, exports the implicit marker from the plugin SDK runtime subpath, and adds QQBot/docs/contract coverage.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +116, Docs +7, Generated 0. Total +170 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main authorizes QQBot approval actions when `execApprovals` is absent, and the current native interaction handler returns that authorization directly. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The protected `maintainer` label, GHSA-linked approval boundary, compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior, and missing real behavior proof make this a maintainer review item rather than an automated repair candidate.
